Epic 4: Dependency Graph — Context-Aware Work Discovery
data.description: Agents and developers can establish directional blocking relationships between issues, query the ready queue, find what's blocking a specific task, visualize the full dependency graph, filter and search issues, and view aggregated workspace metrics. Covers FR8, FR9, FR10, FR11, FR12, FR13.

comments.text: Milestone reached: Story 4.1 (Establish and Remove Dependency Relationships) implementation complete. Added --remove flag, WithAuditedTx, WithDeadlockRetry, lexicographic lock ordering, cycle detection, and structured error codes. PR pending review.

data.title: Story 4.1: Establish and Remove Dependency Relationships
data.description:
As a developer or agent, I want to create directional blocking relationships between issues, so that the system knows which tasks must complete before others can start.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Given issues abc123def456 (blocker) and def456abc123 (blocked) both exist, when I run grava dep --add abc123def456 def456abc123, then a row is inserted in the dependencies table
- The operation uses WithDeadlockRetry to handle concurrent dep writes (ADR-H3)
- grava dep --remove abc123def456 def456abc123 deletes the dependency row
- Attempting to create a circular dependency returns CIRCULAR_DEPENDENCY error
- grava dep --add on a non-existent issue ID returns ISSUE_NOT_FOUND error

FR Coverage: FR8

comments.text: Session Summary (Story 4.1): Refactored addDependency with WithAuditedTx, WithDeadlockRetry, lexicographic lock ordering (ADR-H3), issue existence validation, cycle detection. Implemented removeDependency with audited transactions. Added --remove flag, --json output, EventDependencyRemove constant. 10 new unit tests, all pass. Commit: 17a2b78

data.title: Story 4.2: Query the Ready Queue
data.description:
As an agent, I want to query the top-priority tasks with no active blockers, so that I can immediately identify what work is actionable without manual triage.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Given a workspace with 10 issues (some blocked, some not), when I run grava ready, then only issues with status NOT IN (done, archived) AND no active (non-done) blockers are returned
- Results ordered by priority (high to low) then by created_at ascending
- grava ready --json returns a JSON array conforming to NFR5 schema
- grava ready --limit 5 returns at most 5 issues
- If all open issues are blocked, returns empty array with exit code 0

FR Coverage: FR9

comments.text: Session Summary: Story 4.2 complete. Added ReadyTaskOutput struct with Assignee field (AC#2), fetchAssignees batch query, fixed empty state to use stderr (AC#3). 8 new unit tests covering independent tasks, blocked-by-done, blocked-by-open, empty state JSON/human, assignee in JSON. Commit: ccfbc79

data.title: Story 4.3: Query Blockers for a Specific Issue
data.description:
As a developer or agent, I want to see exactly what upstream issues are blocking a specific task, so that I can resolve blockers in the right order to unblock work.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Given issue def456abc123 is blocked by abc123def456 and xyz789abc123, when I run grava blocked def456abc123, then a list showing each blocker (id, title, status, assignee) is returned
- grava blocked def456abc123 --json returns a JSON array conforming to NFR5 schema
- If the issue has no blockers, returns empty array
- grava blocked on a non-existent issue returns ISSUE_NOT_FOUND error

FR Coverage: FR10

data.title: Story 4.4: Visualize the Full Dependency Graph
data.description:
As a developer or agent, I want to visualize or traverse the overarching dependency structure, so that I can understand the full project topology and identify critical paths.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Given a workspace with interconnected dependency relationships, when I run grava graph, then ASCII-art dependency tree is printed to stdout
- grava graph --format=dot outputs Graphviz DOT-format graph
- grava graph --format=json outputs JSON adjacency list (nodes and edges) conforming to NFR5 schema
- grava graph --root abc123def456 outputs only the subgraph reachable from that issue
- Isolated issues (no deps) appear as standalone nodes, not omitted

FR Coverage: FR11

data.title: Story 4.5: Filter, Search and View Issues
data.description:
As a developer or agent, I want to filter the issue list, search by keyword, and view full details of a single issue, so that I can quickly locate and inspect any work item.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Given a workspace with multiple issues, when I run grava list --status in_progress --assignee agent-01, then only matching issues are returned
- grava search login performs case-insensitive full-text search across title, description, and comments
- grava show abc123def456 displays full issue detail (title, status, priority, assignee, labels, comments, subtasks, dependency info)
- grava show abc123def456 --json returns complete issue object conforming to NFR5 schema
- grava list --json returns JSON array of issue summaries
- All commands return empty results (not errors) when no issues match

FR Coverage: FR12

data.title: Story 4.6: View Workspace Metrics and Stats
data.description:
As a developer, I want to view aggregated workspace performance and status metrics, so that I can assess project health and identify bottlenecks at a glance.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Given a workspace with issues in various states, when I run grava stats, then output includes: total issues by status, count of blocked issues, count of stale in_progress issues (no heartbeat >1h), average cycle time for done issues
- grava stats --json returns structured JSON object conforming to NFR5 schema
- grava stats completes in <100ms at 1K issues (Phase 1 baseline)

FR Coverage: FR13

data.title: Story 3.2: Write and Read Wisp Ephemeral State
Fix --last-commit flag in update and expose last_commit in show --json
data.description: Two bugs fixed: (1) grava update --last-commit fails when used as the only flag because it never registers in changedFields. (2) grava show --json does not include the last_commit field from metadata, making it impossible for skills/agents to retrieve the commit hash programmatically.
